examples.title: E-Waste Collection Algorithm
examples.description: Simple algorithm showing collection and routing of e-waste to appropriate recycling pathway
examples.code:
ALGORITHM E-Waste Collection
BEGIN
 INPUT: Discarded electronic device
 
 IF device is still functional THEN
 SEND to reuse center
 MARK for donation/refurbishment
 ELSE
 SEND to recycling facility
 SORT by device type (CRT, LCD, Battery, PCB)
 
 SWITCH device type
 CASE CRT: Extract leaded glass, send to smelter
 CASE Battery: Send to battery recycler
 CASE PCB: Send to precious metal extractor
 CASE LCD: Extract mercury if present
 END SWITCH
 
 RECOVER materials
 UPDATE inventory of recovered materials
 END IF
 
 GENERATE report of materials recovered
END
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how e-waste is routed based on condition and type, maximizing resource recovery and proper handling of hazardous components

examples.title: EPR Compliance Checker
examples.description: Algorithm to verify if producer meets their Extended Producer Responsibility targets
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Check EPR Compliance
BEGIN
 INPUT: Producer_ID, Units_Sold, Units_Collected
 
 SET target_percentage = 30 // Minimum 30% collection target
 
 CALCULATE required_collection = (Units_Sold * target_percentage) / 100
 
 IF Units_Collected >= required_collection THEN
 SET status = "COMPLIANT"
 CALCULATE bonus_points = Units_Collected - required_collection
 ELSE
 SET status = "NON-COMPLIANT"
 CALCULATE deficit = required_collection - Units_Collected
 CALCULATE penalty = deficit * fine_per_unit
 END IF
 
 OUTPUT: status, bonus_points OR penalty
 SEND notification to producer
END
examples.explanation: Shows how producers are tracked for meeting e-waste collection obligations under EPR rules, with penalties for non-compliance
